chunks.title: Heading, Paragraphs and Links
chunks.text: In today's video, I will tell you about heading and paragraphs. Along with that, I will also tell you about anchor text. If you have found this course awesome till now, then do like this video. Your likes will help me in bringing such free courses.

chunks.title: Heading, Paragraphs and Links
chunks.text: In this video, we will make a small project, which will be a bookmark website. This bookmark website will help you to bring all your favorite websites in one place. You will be able to use this and this in actual.

chunks.title: Heading, Paragraphs and Links
chunks.text: So let's start quickly. Write down in the comment quickly, Sigma Batch OP. Let's roll the intro. So guys, as you can see, I have come to my computer screen.

chunks.title: Heading, Paragraphs and Links
chunks.text: And let's say I write code with Harry here. So you must have seen such titles. I will write h1 here. Now what happens with h1? You can add a heading to your web page with h1.

chunks.title: Heading, Paragraphs and Links
chunks.text: We talked about the basic structure. We said that this tells that this is an HTML page. HTML lang is equal to en. Lang is an attribute and its value is en. This means that the content of this page is in English.

chunks.title: Heading, Paragraphs and Links
chunks.text: This tells the search engines. Or if anyone is sitting with the link of this web page, then he will know that this web page is in English. Accordingly, he can parse this web page. The browser will also know that your web page is in English.

chunks.title: Heading, Paragraphs and Links
chunks.text: Now there is a head here, which has metadata, which title of the page, which CSS is linked, all these things are information. The main content is inside the body. Now there is a tag inside the body to put a heading,

chunks.title: Heading, Paragraphs and Links
chunks.text: whose name is h1. And I am going to write my bookmarks on it. So if I write my bookmarks here, then we will write it with the h1 tag. And if I want to make it live here,

chunks.title: Heading, Paragraphs and Links
chunks.text: I will click here. And after that I will open it in the browser. So see, this is how my bookmarks look like. Now why should we always use h1? Why didn't I write my bookmark directly?

chunks.title: Heading, Paragraphs and Links
chunks.text: Because I am telling the browser that this is my heading. And this is my first level heading. Now suppose I want to put two types of bookmarks in my bookmarks. One main bookmark and one secondary bookmark. So I will use h2, i.e. the second level heading.

chunks.title: Heading, Paragraphs and Links
chunks.text: So I will write here. If you have used Microsoft Word, then you will know what h1 and h2 are. But even if you don't know, then I will tell you that h1 is your main level heading.

chunks.title: Heading, Paragraphs and Links
chunks.text: And after that, the second level heading is from h2. Suppose I say main bookmarks and secondary bookmarks. Suppose I do something like this. And here I will press Alt, Shift and Down Arrow. Let me do one thing.

chunks.text: Why don't I turn on screencast mode? So what did I do here? I pressed Alt, Shift and Down Arrow. So this is replicated. This is the shortcut of VS Code.

chunks.text: I would like to tell you one more shortcut of VS Code. If you press Alt on your keyboard, and then wherever you click, a cursor will be created there. Suppose if I click here,

chunks.text: then only one cursor will come. But by pressing Alt, if I click somewhere else, then the cursor will keep coming there. Let's say I want to do all bookmarks,
